name: Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas
name_original: Acequia del Toril y dólmenes de Alicún – circular por Fonelas
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Las Torres traverse diverse landscapes within the Granada Geopark, characterized by desert terrain, unique geological formations, and river confluence areas. The region features natural attractions like the Acequia del Toril, a naturally formed irrigation channel, and the Waterfall of Alicún with its distinct mineral deposits. Mountain bikers can explore routes that wind through arid badlands and past prehistoric archaeological sites, offering varied riding experiences. The area's elevation ranges from 700-800 meters, providing a mix of climbs and descents across its natural surroundings. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Las Torres The most popular mountain bike trail is Gorafe Desert Trail, a moderate 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through the striking desert landscapes of Gorafe. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los Colorados – circular desde Villanueva de las Torres por el Desierto de Gorafe, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This circular route explores the unique badlands and desert formations around Villanueva de las Torres. Local mountain bikers also love the Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas, a 22.6 miles (36.4 km) trail leading through geological features and prehistoric sites, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Las Torres is defined by desert landscapes, unique geological formations, and prehistoric archaeological sites. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Villanueva De Las Torres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. Nearly 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Las Torres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villanueva De Las Torres?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails documented around Villanueva De Las Torres on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Villanueva De Las Torres?
A: Mountain biking in Villanueva De Las Torres primarily features diverse desert landscapes within the Granada Geopark. You'll encounter unique geological formations, arid badlands, and river confluence areas, providing a distinct riding experience.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in the area?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy mountain bike trail available around Villanueva De Las Torres, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Additionally, there are 8 moderate trails that can be suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the mountain bike routes?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical sights. You can explore the unique geological formation of the Acequia del Toril, a naturally formed irrigation channel, or discover prehistoric archaeological sites. Many routes also traverse the stunning desert landscapes of the Granada Geopark, offering views of badlands and canyons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2793795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Canyons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155646931/los-colorados-circular-desde-villanueva-de-las-torres-por-el-desierto-de-gorafe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Colorados – circular desde Villanueva de las Torres por el Desierto de Gorafe&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the unique badlands and desert formations. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155320759/acequia-del-toril-and-dolmens-of-alicun-circular-route-through-fonelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas&lt;/a&gt;, leading through geological features and prehistoric sites.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villanueva De Las Torres?
A: The mountain bike routes around Villanueva De Las Torres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. Nearly 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique desert landscapes and geological formations.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area offers several scenic viewpoints. You might encounter spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive views over the desert landscapes and geological formations of the Granada Geopark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villanueva De Las Torres?
A: Given the desert climate of the Granada Geopark, the best time for mountain biking is typically during the cooler months, such as spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons more suitable if riding during that season. Winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Waterfall of Alicún?
A: While specific mountain bike trails directly to the Waterfall of Alicún are not detailed, the waterfall is a notable natural feature near the thermal baths of Alicún. Routes in the general vicinity, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155320759/acequia-del-toril-and-dolmens-of-alicun-circular-route-through-fonelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas&lt;/a&gt;, explore the geological and historical richness of the area, and you may find opportunities to visit the waterfall as a side trip.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult mountain bike routes available around Villanueva De Las Torres. These trails often feature more significant elevation changes and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 48 km with over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in this region?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Las Torres vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013299548/gorafe-desert-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.5 km route that takes about 2 hours and 31 minutes. Longer routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Escalerilla loop from Pedro Martínez&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to over 44 km and take more than 4 hours to complete.
tips: The Acequia del Toril is a porous limestone aqueduct, more than 1.5 km long and more than 10 meters high at some points. Its natural construction over millennia has been used since prehistoric times to supply a settlement from one of the eight nearby hot springs. This meandering geological formation, with walls up to three meters thick, remains an impressive testament to natural engineering and human use of water.
Beautiful gravel road through the steppe landscape. The plateau is breathtaking, as are the views at the edge of the many valleys in the area. Beautiful as a descent and as a climb.
